metadata.rationale: The article invents the central news event: there is no evidence here that Orlando City dismissed Oscar Pareja or that MLS opened a formal review over remarks to Messi. It further uses a generated press-conference image and misframes ordinary match visuals as proof of a disciplinary fallout they do not actually document.

The text claims that Orlando City dismissed head coach Oscar Pareja following a match against Inter Miami. The image shows a press conference setting with clear branding for "Orlando City" and "Orlando Health". The nameplate on the table in front of the seated man clearly reads "OSCAR PAREJA HEAD COACH". This visual evidence directly confirms the identity of the coach mentioned in the text and his role at the club, supporting the narrative that he was the subject of the hiring/firing decision. The image establishes him as the Head Coach, consistent with the text's description.

The news text explicitly identifies "head coach Oscar Pareja" as the subject of the dismissal. To verify this cross-modal matching, I will use the Entity extraction tool to confirm if the name and title appear on the desk in the image.
